description: Quadcopter drone chassis in X configuration, flat on XY plane. Central hub disc with four diagonal arms at 45/135/225/315 degrees, each terminating in a circular motor mount platform with shaft hole. M3 mounting holes in hub.
components: Motor mount NE (18mm dia, centered at ~(51.6, 51.6, 1.5))
Motor mount NW (18mm dia, centered at ~(-51.6, 51.6, 1.5))
Motor mount SW (18mm dia, centered at ~(-51.6, -51.6, 1.5))
Motor mount SE (18mm dia, centered at ~(51.6, -51.6, 1.5))
Motor shaft hole NE (5mm dia through mount)
Motor shaft hole NW (5mm dia through mount)
Motor shaft hole SW (5mm dia through mount)
Motor shaft hole SE (5mm dia through mount)

features.description: 8 hole(s) of diameter 5.0mm (motor shaft) and 3.2mm (M3 mounting) mm

notes: 1. All geometry lies in Z=0 to Z=3 (flat plate). 2. Arms start at hub edge (r=18mm) and end at r=73mm along each 45-deg diagonal. Each arm rectangle is 55mm x 10mm, aligned along its diagonal direction — use a rotated workplane or a rotated box primitive for each arm. 3. Motor mount circles (r=9mm) are centered exactly at (±73*cos(45°), ±73*sin(45°)) = (±51.619, ±51.619); verify the mount fully covers the arm tip (arm width 10mm < mount diameter 18mm, so mount overhangs arm slightly on sides — this is intentional). 4. Boolean union all solid bodies (hub + 4 arms + 4 mounts) before cutting holes to avoid face-on-face artifacts. 5. Motor shaft holes (5mm dia) and M3 holes (3.2mm dia) are through-holes in Z direction (full 3mm depth). 6. The overall bounding box diagonal tip-to-tip is 2 * 73mm * cos(45°) + 18mm = ~164mm square. 7. Volume estimate: hub ~3054mm³ + 4 arms ~(55*10*3=1650 each, minus hub overlap ~(18*10*3=540 each overlap area approx)) + 4 mounts ~(π*9²*3≈763 each) minus holes; approximate net solid ~9800mm³. Coder should use CadQuery or similar; union then subtract holes.
